basic_info.headline: Senior Staff Research Scientist@Google DeepMind(ex@Anthropic)
basic_info.about:
Gemini post training research. co-lead gemini-ascent project on research automation.

I was a research scientist at Anthropic on reinforcement learning. Things I contribute to:
--with Vihan Jain: building the RL fundamental team
--Claude 3.7 Sonnet
--basic RL science behind Claude 4 family

 I was a postdoctoral researcher at UC Berkeley.  I worked on dynamics in quantum systems in the past. My main contribution to science including:
1. Understanding quantum many body chaos, especially the 'scramblon' mode at semiclassical regime and various phenomena related to it.(Collaboration with Douglas Stanford and Zhenbin Yang, and later with Phil Saad and Shreya Vardhan)
2. Understanding open quantum systems, especially discovery of the 'non-Hermitian Skin effect' with Zhong Wang.(Later with Fei Song)
For my publications, see: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=i4kyLbwAAAAJ

experience.title: Teaching Assistant(PhD)
experience.description: Teaching assistant for Phys 212 Graduate Statistical Mechanics; Phys 230&231 Graduate Quantum Mechanics I&II; Physics 234 Advanced topics in Quantum Mechanics; Phys 331 Quantum Field Theory II

education.degree: Doctor of Philosophy - PhD, Theoretical and Mathematical Physics
education.description: Research in dynamical properties of quantum field theory and quantum gravity, and its relation to quantum information and mathematics. Co-advised by professor Douglas Stanford and Stephen Shenker
education.skills: Julia (Programming Language), Python (Programming Language) and +4 skills

education.degree: Bachelor of Science - BS, Condensed Matter and Materials Physics
education.description: Research in theoretical condensed matter physics. Specifically about open quantum systems, topological phases. Discovered 'Non-hermitian Skin Effect'. Advised by professor Zhong Wang at IAS, Tsinghua University.
education.activities: Tsinghua presidential award(清华大学特等奖学金, highest award for undergraduates at Tsinghua University). Chi-Sun Yeh Prize, Tsinghua University
education.skills: Quantum Mechanics, Numerical Simulation and +3 skills
